item.title: ME:: Self hosting is difficult for geeks too. Most geeks should pay for managed hosting instead ; Neil Brown:: 'Self-host it' is not the answer because it's exclusionary
item.description: Discovered: Mar 6, 2026 16:17 (UTC) ME:: Self hosting is difficult for geeks too. Most geeks should pay for managed hosting instead ; Neil Brown:: ‘Self-host it’ is not the answer because it’s exclusionary

item.title: How to unfollow everyone in LinkedIn by running a JS script in your browser console ; Ton Zijlstra:: 2021:: Emptied My LinkedIn Feed
item.description: Discovered: Mar 6, 2026 16:06 (UTC) How to unfollow everyone in LinkedIn by running a JS script in your browser console ; Ton Zijlstra:: 2021:: Emptied My LinkedIn Feed <– German Rodrigo’s 2020 blog post about the Javascript script and a Chrome (and I guess by now Firefox) extension <– QUOTE: To get to an empty timeline I had to unfollow everyone I’m connected to. Which is not a simple thing to do, as LinkedIn provides no easy option to unfollow large amounts of people, and requires you to unfollow everyone one by one. Of course there are work arounds and that is what I used, with a snippet of code in my browser console.

item.title: I would encourage Filipinos worldwide not to gate keep & judge the accents of Perlah, Princess & Trinity on The Pitt
item.description:
• Draft created: Mar 6, 2026 15:58 (UTC).
  • I would encourage Filipinos worldwide not to gate keep & judge the accents of Perlah, Princess & Trinity on The Pitt. I’ve known immigrants who lost their ability to speak in <2 years, others who still have an accent after 20 years, & others who seamlessly switch between English & their Filipino language(s) in the middle of a sentence even after 20 years. Fluency is prismatic not a binary switch :-) Of course it’s not just Filipinos! It happens to German, French speaking immigrants, etc
  • And I’ve never met a Filipino born in the USA or Canada who speaks any Filipino languages fluently but that’s because I am old :-) and there weren’t many Filipino kids I met growing up in small town Ontario, Canada in the 70s and 80s. There are many such people now and for at least the last 20 years in Toronto, Vancouver and California and probably everywhere else there are lots of us :-)

item.title: Eat. Sleep. Wakeup. Work :-) These words are some of the few I know in Tagalog :-) OPM:: YouTube:: Gracenote performs 'TAONG ROBOT (KAIN. TULOG. GISING. TRABAHO)' LIVE on Wish 107.5 Bus
item.description: Discovered: Mar 3, 2026 07:40 (UTC) Eat. Sleep. Wakeup. Work :-) These words are some of the few I know in Tagalog :-) OPM:: YouTube:: Gracenote performs “TAONG ROBOT (KAIN. TULOG. GISING. TRABAHO)” LIVE on Wish 107.5 Bus

item.title: Go Oliver go! I coached Oliver when he was 5 or 6 at the Hollyburn Cross Country Ski Club and I'm super proud to humble brag he finished first in his age group 13-15 with a VERY fast time of 39:06 at the 2026 Payak 15km cross country ski 'event' on February 28, 2026) ¦ Zone4.ca
item.description: Discovered: Mar 2, 2026 15:32 (UTC) Go Oliver go! I coached Oliver when he was 5 or 6 at the Hollyburn Cross Country Ski Club and I’m super proud to humble brag he finished first in his age group 13-15 with a VERY fast time of 39:06 at the 2026 Payak 15km cross country ski ‘event’ on February 28, 2026) ¦ Zone4.ca <- all of my former Hollyburn Cross Country Ski Jack Rabbits and Jack Bunnies will always be winners!!!!!!! 7 years of winners!

item.title: Keir Archibald and Matthew Dormer developed a un-detectable magnetic driven bicycle, 'e-motor doping' is real 'I believe' :-)
item.description: Discovered: Mar 1, 2026 16:32 (UTC) Keir Archibald and Matthew Dormer developed a un-detectable magnetic driven bicycle, ‘e-motor doping’ is real ‘I believe’ :-) <– QUOTE: We developed the first ever concealed electromagnetic drive system integrated into a top-level racing bicycle, engineered to deliver an additional 20W while remaining undetectable under most existing UCI inspection methods. <- YouTube Video on GCN: Motor Doping IS Possible. We’ve Done It.

item.title: Payak 2026 1:49, 3rd last in my age group :-) twas a good day :-) Results - 2026 Coast Outdoors P’ayakenstat (Payak) ¦ Zone4.ca
item.description:
Discovered: Mar 1, 2026 01:41 (UTC) Payak 2026 1:49, 3rd last in my age group :-) twas a good day :-) Results - 2026 Coast Outdoors P’ayakenstat (Payak) ¦ Zone4.ca

Finished 54th (out of 60) overall LOL in “Male 18+, 15km” 3rd last in my age group :-)

  • Split time was good! 50:59.9
  • Finish time: 1:49:23.6 +1:04:30.1
  • Free15 km Male (60-64): 10/13
  • https://www.strava.com/activities/17557264310

Previously

  • 2016: 1:52, flickr screenshot in case sports tracker goes away :-), Chip time:: 1:49
  • 2017 Payak 2017 - 1:25 instead of 1:49 Why? Better skis, better wax, faster track, better snow, Chip time: 1:40
  • 2018 Payak 2018: 1 hour 40 minutes instead of 1:25, -4 degrees, not as fast, skate skiing uphill technique needs work, Chip time: 1:40
  • 2019 Chip time: 1:29
  • 2020 Payak 2020: 1:40 oops fell 3 times in the first 5km, but mountain view climb was fast because of the klaebo running up hill diagonal stride technique, Chip time: 1:40
  • 2023 Payak 2023: 1:28 I thought I was fastest evah but I wasn’t. The cold (-8) and unwisely stopping just before the end for a drink (oops) made me slower, Chip time: 1:28
  • 2024, Didn’t participate in Payak 2024 because there was no snow at Cypress and I didn’t xc ski much (under 10 times before March 1, 2024)
  • 2025, Chip time: 1:43

Strava embed

item.title: ME:: tl;dr-ing: ATProto is the magical federated mesh. Best of federated + mesh worlds or worst :-) ? ; Paul Frazee:: Practical Decentralization ¦ Paul's Dev Notes
item.description:
Discovered: Feb 28, 2026 05:38 (UTC) ME:: tl;dr-ing: ATProto is the magical federated mesh. Best of federated + mesh worlds or worst :-) ? ; Paul Frazee:: Practical Decentralization ¦ Paul’s Dev Notes

QUOTE

The magical federated mesh

The atproto is both models and neither. More specifically, it’s a hybrid.

item.title: ME:: УSA: Broken Rich Crook Scene :-) aka 'No Rich Crook Left Behind' LOL ; Adam Serwer:: How America Chose Not to Hold the Powerful to Account - The Atlantic
item.description: Discovered: Feb 28, 2026 05:15 (UTC) ME:: УSA: Broken Rich Crook Scene :-) aka ‘No Rich Crook Left Behind’ LOL ; Adam Serwer:: How America Chose Not to Hold the Powerful to Account - The Atlantic <– QUOTE: Instead of a New Deal, we have a Great Society for white-collar crime, a New Frontier of executive lawbreaking, a No Rich Crook Left Behind. Most of us probably don’t even realize it. Nevertheless, this has been the priority for the wealthy and powerful, who have managed to convince a critical mass of Americans that they will be able to enjoy the same privileges. They won’t.
